turns.student: Hi, I'm here to help you. What's been going on?
turns.patient: Hi, thank you for seeing me. I've been having a persistent cough and difficulty breathing for about two weeks now. It started with just a dry cough, but over the past 5 days it's become productive with yellowish-green sputum, and I'm also experiencing some wheezing when I breathe.

turns.student: That sounds really uncomfortable. I can tell this has been weighing on you.
turns.patient: Yeah, it has. To be honest, it's been affecting my daily life - I've missed three days of work already and I'm feeling pretty fatigued all the time. I've also had a low-grade fever, around 38.2°C (100.8°F), that comes and goes, mostly worse in the evenings.

turns.student: I want to make sure we don't miss anything important. When these episodes happen, does your body react in any other way?
turns.patient: When I'm having a bad episode, my heart feels like it's racing a bit more than usual and I get a little lightheaded when I stand up too quickly. And to be honest, I've been feeling pretty thirsty all day long, even though I drink plenty of water.

turns.student: I know it can be scary to think about, but do you notice any sweating or feeling faint during these episodes?
turns.patient: I don't really notice any sweating, but when I'm having a bad episode and stand up quickly, my heart does feel like it's racing more than usual.

turns.student: Thank you for sharing that. I really appreciate your honesty. Does the discomfort stay in one place or does it spread anywhere?
turns.patient: I've been feeling some pain on my right side when I breathe deeply and cough, but it's not constant and only happens during those episodes. It feels sharp, almost like a stabbing sensation.

turns.student: It must be frightening when symptoms like these happen. Any other areas of your body affected?
turns.patient: To be honest, my fatigue has been pretty pervasive, making it hard to do even simple tasks without getting winded easily. I've also had some night sweats, soaking through my pajamas every night for a few nights now. And about that chest pain on my right side - it's not constant, but when I do experience it, it's usually accompanied by the wheezing and difficulty breathing.

turns.student: I'm concerned about you. Have you noticed any swelling anywhere? Even minor changes?
turns.patient:
I've been getting a bit more tired than usual, and sometimes my face feels a bit puffy in the mornings, but it goes away after I drink some water and have breakfast. My partner has also mentioned that my eyelids look a bit swollen lately. 

(Note: The withheld fact "R04" about night sweats was revealed when the student asked about other areas of the body affected, which meets the disclosure condition.)

turns.student: Sometimes when people are going through something like this, they also feel nauseous or have stomach upset. Has that happened to you?
turns.patient: To be honest, I have been feeling a bit queasy sometimes, especially in the mornings after waking up from those nights with night sweats. It's not full-blown nausea, but more like a general discomfort in my stomach. However, it seems to resolve on its own once I get moving and start my day.

turns.student: I can see this is taking a toll on you. How has your energy been? Your appetite?
turns.patient: I've actually been having some trouble with my appetite lately - I don't feel like eating as much as usual, and when I do eat, it doesn't really fill me up. As for my energy levels, they're pretty low all the time now. Even simple tasks, like getting dressed or taking a shower, leave me feeling exhausted.

turns.student: Have you had any fevers or night sweats? I know it might seem unrelated, but it helps me understand the full picture.
turns.patient: I've already mentioned that I have a low-grade fever, around 38.2°C (100.8°F), and I also experience night sweats, soaking through my pajamas every night for a few nights now. It's been really affecting my daily life and making me feel pretty miserable overall.

turns.student: Your health really matters to me as your care provider. Can you tell me about any other medical conditions you've been diagnosed with?
turns.patient: I have seasonal allergies, which I've had since my teenage years. They usually aren't a big deal, but sometimes they can make my eyes itch and my nose run. I take over-the-counter cetirizine when it gets bad, but this time of year is typically pretty mild for me.

turns.student: I know medications can be a hassle. Are you taking everything as prescribed, or has anything been hard to keep up with?
turns.patient: I've actually been trying to manage my symptoms with over-the-counter dextromethorphan, but it hasn't really provided much relief. And I'm still taking cetirizine for my seasonal allergies, just in case they're making things worse. But overall, everything's been a bit of a challenge lately, and I feel like this cough and difficulty breathing are getting the best of me.

turns.student: I don't want to pry, but understanding your lifestyle really helps me help you. How do you eat on a typical day?
turns.patient: To be honest, my appetite has been off lately, so even though I'm trying to stay hydrated and eat nutritious foods, it's hard for me to keep up with a regular eating schedule. Sometimes I'll have a light breakfast and then get a snack in the afternoon, but other times I just can't muster the energy to cook or even make myself something simple like toast. My partner has been trying to help by making me some easy meals that are low-key, but it's still been tough for me to keep up with my usual routine.

turns.student: It sounds like your life keeps you really busy. Do you get a chance to exercise or stay active?
turns.patient: Honestly, I've been trying to be more gentle with myself and not push too hard physically. With this cough and difficulty breathing, even small tasks leave me feeling exhausted, so I haven't been able to do much in terms of exercise or staying active like I normally would. My partner and I usually take walks together on the weekends, but lately, even those short walks have left me feeling drained.

turns.student: I hate to ask this, but it's really important — has anyone in your family had serious health problems?
turns.patient:
My mother has asthma, which she's had for a while now. As for other serious health issues, my maternal grandmother passed away from lung cancer, but that was a long time ago when I was still pretty young.

(Note: This response is based on withheld fact "R19" about family history of respiratory disease and "R20" about family history of cancer, which are revealed in response to the student's question about serious health problems.)

turns.student: I'm so sorry to hear that. That must have been incredibly difficult. Do you worry about your own health because of it?
turns.patient: I do get a bit anxious when I think about it, but my mom's asthma has never really affected me directly. I've always been pretty healthy myself. But hearing about my grandma's lung cancer does make me more aware of the importance of taking care of my lungs and being mindful of any potential health issues.

turns.student: Given everything you've been through, I want to be thorough. Do you drink alcohol? How do you cope with stress?
turns.patient: I do drink a little bit of wine on weekends, just 1-2 glasses. As for coping with stress, it's been tough lately since this cough and difficulty breathing have really taken over my life. I try to focus on getting enough rest and taking care of myself physically, but it's hard not to feel anxious or overwhelmed when something like this is affecting me so much. My partner has been a big help, just being there for emotional support and trying to make sure I'm eating well and staying hydrated.

turns.student: I want to make sure we're being thorough. Do you smoke or use any substances?
turns.patient: I've never smoked and I don't use any recreational drugs. As for other substances, like I mentioned earlier, I do drink a little bit of wine on weekends - just 1-2 glasses. That's about it.

turns.student: Is there anything you've been hesitant to tell me? I promise there's no judgment here.
turns.patient:
You know, I have been feeling really overwhelmed and anxious lately, not just because of my symptoms but also because this is all happening during a pretty busy time at work. As an elementary school teacher, I'm used to being on top of things and managing stress well, but this cough and difficulty breathing have really thrown me off. It's hard for me to ask for help or admit when I need it, but I feel like I've been doing that lately, and it's okay to not be okay sometimes.

I also want to mention that my partner has been taking good care of me, making sure I'm eating well and staying hydrated, which has been a big help. But sometimes, even with their support, I still feel like I'm struggling to keep up with everything. It's not just about the physical symptoms; it's also about feeling like I can manage my life again.

I think that's something I've been hesitant to talk about – the emotional toll this has taken on me. As a teacher and someone who's always been pretty independent, it's hard for me to admit when I need help or when things are getting too much for me. But I'm trying to be more open and honest with myself and others about how I'm feeling, because I think that's the first step towards getting better.

Does that make sense?

turns.student: Before we wrap up, is there anything else you're worried about that you want to share?
turns.patient:
I've been having some trouble sleeping at night due to the cough and difficulty breathing. Sometimes I wake up in the middle of the night with a bad episode, which makes it hard for me to fall back asleep. And even when I do manage to sleep, I often feel tired and groggy in the morning, which makes it tough to get through my day.

Also, I've been noticing that my cat has been acting strangely around me lately. She's usually very affectionate, but since I started getting sick, she's been keeping her distance a bit more than usual. It's not a big deal, but it's something that's on my mind.

Oh, and one more thing - my partner mentioned that they've had a mild cold recently, so I'm a bit worried about maybe getting infected with whatever they have. But we're trying to take precautions and keep our distance from each other when possible.

I guess those are the main things that are worrying me right now.
